title: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers vote to join union
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers in Cambridge voted to unionize with UFCW Local 1445 to negotiate their first contract.
The union will address issues like wages, benefits, health care, and workplace safety at Lamplighter's two locations.
The unionization follows Lamplighter's decade-long operation, joining a broader trend among restaurant and bar industry workers.

title: Pedestrian hospitalized after being struck by MBTA bus in Norwood
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 35-year-old woman was hospitalized after being struck by an MBTA bus in Norwood on Monday.
The incident occurred around 3:45 p.m. on Washington Street; the woman was outside of a marked crosswalk.
The woman, conscious and alert when transported, is in stable condition; transit police are investigating.

title: A Mass. town voted down a new fire station. The old one just got shut down after the blizzard.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Westwood, MA Fire Station 1 shut down after blizzard damage, forcing relocation of its operations to another station.
Voters rejected two proposals to fund a new $38.1 million fire station last year, prolonging infrastructural issues.
Temporary fire operation arrangements are being made as town officials await estimates for structural repairs.

title: He’s running his 130th marathon at the 130th Boston Marathon
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Alberto Colin, 60, from Long Beach, California, plans his 130th marathon to coincide with the 130th Boston Marathon.
Colin has run the Boston Marathon consecutively since 2011 and designed milestones during the COVID-19 shutdown.
He aims to inspire others with his story and highlights the Boston Marathon's impact on runners globally.

title: A new six-story building could rise where a pizza shop and dentist now stand in Jamaica Plain
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: VinCo Properties plans a six-story building with 35 apartments and retail at 3716-3724 Washington St. in Jamaica Plain.
The development will replace Forest Hills Pizza and Dental, requiring Zoning Board relief for building dimensions and parking.
The Boston Planning Board will hold a public meeting about the proposal on March 11.

title: Jayson Tatum to doctor: ‘I ain’t coming back to be no role player’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum intends to return as a key player, not a role player, following his Achilles' tendon surgery.
Tatum was cleared by Dr. Martin O'Malley for return, with Celtics awaiting his readiness for full participation.
Celtics have maintained success without Tatum, with Jaylen Brown thriving and young players stepping up.

title: ‘You should’ve been a Celtic’: Julius Erving on Red Auerbach, rivalry with the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Julius Erving discussed Red Auerbach's dislike for the ABA and a humorous remark about playing for the Celtics.
Erving reflected on the longstanding rivalry with the Celtics, noting it began before his iconic fights with Larry Bird.
Despite rivalry tensions, Erving and Bird had interactions off-court, including posing for an Atari video game cover.

title: Boston named finalist to host 2028 Democratic National Convention
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston named one of five finalists to host the 2028 Democratic National Convention by the DNC.
Site visits for Boston, Atlanta, Chicago, Denver, and Philadelphia scheduled for spring to assess convention bids.
Hosting the convention could bring up to $400 million in economic benefit to Massachusetts.

title: Boston-area hospitals rank among the world’s best, Newsweek says
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fourteen Massachusetts hospitals feature on Newsweek's 2026 global hospital ranking, including two in the top 20.
Massachusetts General Hospital in Boston is ranked fifth, with Brigham and Women's Hospital ranked 18th.
Newsweek's hospital list evaluated over 2,500 institutions using public data and surveys from medical experts and patients.

title: Here’s what Jon Stewart said about Trump’s decision to go to war with Iran
author: John Waller
bullets: Jon Stewart criticized Trump's decision to go to war with Iran, questioning the motives and implications.
Stewart expressed concern over the potential human and financial cost of the conflict with Iran.
The comedian's comments highlighted a broader debate about the U.S. military's role in the Middle East.
